description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from dark, weathered stone blocks, forming a robust and symmetrical archway. The arch is composed of evenly spaced, rectangular voussoirs that create a pronounced keystone effect at the apex. The base consists of two solid, rectangular plinths that anchor the structure and provide visual stability. The surface of the arch displays a rough, aged texture, suggesting exposure to the elements and a sense of historical significance. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, giving the arch a utilitarian and austere appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of Roman architectural styles, emphasizing strength and durability.
description: A dark stone triumphal arch with a rough, blocky texture and solid rectangular bases, featuring a simple, utilitarian Roman design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway with its blocky voussoirs and solid rectangular bases.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile and thickness of the arch, with the stone blocks stacked vertically.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the archway and bases with similar block arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and vertical stone stacking.
